name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical method development, multi-technique materials characterization, and quality-focused process development. Expertise in chromatographic techniques (HPLC, GC-MS, GPC), spectroscopic methods (FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, NMR), and thermal analysis (TGA, DSC) for small molecule and polymer systems. Proficient in statistical data analysis (DOE, SPC, JMP) and experienced in method validation, specification development, and cross-functional technical communication. Strong track record in cGMP-aligned laboratory management and documentation.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Leading analytical method development for polymer process development, designing HPLC, GPC, and spectroscopic protocols to monitor reaction progress, assess product purity, and establish quality control specifications for scale-up.
Developing and validating thermal analysis methods (TGA, DSC) for thermal stability assessment and degradation profiling of bio-based polymer materials.
Applying statistical experimental design (DOE) to identify critical process parameters, optimize reaction conditions, and establish reproducible process control specifications.

Developed and executed multi-technique analytical characterization protocols (FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, ellipsometry) to evaluate material properties and identify failure mechanisms in 15+ organic and organometallic compound variants.

Designed and performed accelerated stability studies under controlled stress conditions, analyzing degradation pathways and impurity profiles to inform material selection and process optimization decisions.
Authored comprehensive technical reports communicating analytical findings, root cause analyses, and design recommendations to cross-functional teams of chemists and engineers.

Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for molecular thin-film coatings, developing surface preparation protocols and CVD deposition strategies with in-situ analytical monitoring to achieve controlled film morphology and reproducible device performance.

Developed and optimized polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring, establishing process control parameters and characterizing film growth kinetics to improve coating reproducibility.

Managed analytical instrumentation and process development laboratory operations for a 15-member research group; implemented equipment tracking, calibration, and qualification systems improving instrument uptime by 30%; developed SOPs for method execution, data documentation, and safety protocols aligned with best practices for analytical data integrity.

skills.Analytical Techniques: HPLC, GC-MS, GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, NMR, TGA, DSC, ellipsometry, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, QCM-D
skills.Method Development: Analytical method development and validation, impurity profiling, specification setting, stability testing, assay development, dissolution testing (familiarity)
skills.Statistical Tools: DOE, Statistical Process Control (SPC), JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel (advanced), Weibull analysis (familiarity)
skills.Quality & Regulatory: cGMP awareness, SOP development, data integrity principles, technical documentation, cross-functional communication
skills.Materials Expertise: Small molecule characterization, polymer analysis, thin-film materials, organic/organometallic compounds, surface analysis

projects.title: Analytical Method Development for Polymer Quality Control
projects.description: Led development of chromatographic and spectroscopic methods for monitoring polymer synthesis, characterizing product quality, and establishing release specifications for novel bio-based polymer materials.
projects.tech: HPLC, GPC, FT-IR, TGA, DSC, DOE

projects.title: Multi-Variant Material Reliability Assessment
projects.description: Designed and executed systematic analytical evaluation of 15+ material variants using accelerated stability testing and multi-technique characterization to identify degradation pathways and inform material selection.
projects.tech: UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, Cyclic Voltammetry, Statistical Analysis

projects.title: AFOSR Thin-Film Coatings Process Development
projects.description: Co-led process development for defense optical coating application, developing deposition protocols with analytical monitoring to ensure consistent film quality and performance.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
